100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 25, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as a coal miners' strike continued, disrupting the economy and intensifying class tensions. In the United States, the Senate debated the potential ratification of the World Court, highlighting America's ongoing struggle with international engagement. Meanwhile, in Italy, Mussolini's regime cracked down on dissent, quelling opposition with increased police presence. Across the globe, the League of Nations worked to address tensions in the Middle East, as unrest in Palestine persisted.
Updated: Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 07:32:00 AM CDT
